mnemonics: D.C. Rep-Guard: Defending Computers with Reputation-Guided Action, Ready & Prepared
keyPoints: Reputation-Guided Protection uses continuous monitoring and behavioral analysis to assign a trust score to data center components.
It shifts security from a static, perimeter-based model to a dynamic, adaptive one that can identify threats from within.
The system can automatically isolate or restrict components with a low reputation score, preventing lateral movement by attackers.
commonMistakes: Confusing it with traditional signature-based antivirus software; it's a behavioral and trust-based system.
Assuming a high reputation score guarantees a component is safe; sophisticated attacks can mimic normal behavior to maintain a good reputation.
